digest.en: FIFA faces criticism for awarding a prize to Donald Trump. The Sherpas have cleared the Mount Everest summit route after a two-week blockage. Iranian leaders say the US can no longer dictate policy to other nations.
digest.hi: नेतन्याहू को सत्ता से हटाने के लिए दो धुर विरोधी नेताओं ने हाथ मिलाया है। इस बीच, ईरान ने होर्मुज जलडमरूमध्य को खोलने के लिए अमेरिका से नाकेबंदी हटाने की मांग की है। अमेरिका ने इस प्रस्ताव को ठुकरा दिया है।

articles.title: Before World Cup, FIFA slammed for politicising sport with Trump prize
articles.summary: FIFA faces criticism for awarding its Peace Prize to US President Donald Trump. Football officials and players question the decision. Football officials and players question FIFA’s decision to award its inaugural Peace Prize to US President Donald Trump.

articles.title: Mount Everest summit route cleared by Sherpas after 2-week blockage
articles.summary: Sherpas cleared the Mount Everest summit route after a 2-week blockage. Mount Everest summit route cleared by Sherpas after 2-week blockage.

articles.title: Iran says U.S. no longer in position to ‘dictate’ policy to other nations
articles.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister praised Russia ties and welcomed Moscow's support for diplomacy amid US tensions. U. S., Iran clash over Tehran's nuclear programme as review of atomic treaty begins at UN; Iranian Foreign Minister praises Russia ties, welcomes Moscow's support for diplomacy.

articles.title: Fuel price surge from Iran war squeezes Philippine fisherfolk
articles.summary: Rising oil prices cut into incomes for 2 million Philippine fisherfolk as costs climb and catches fall. Rising oil prices are cutting into incomes for more than 2 million people across the country’s fishing sector, as costs climb and catches fall.

articles.title: Mexican special forces arrest top commander of powerful cartel
articles.summary: Mexican special forces arrested Audias Flores, a top Jalisco New Generation Cartel commander, in Nayarit.

articles.title: In war with Israel, Hezbollah shows it is still powerful
articles.summary: Hezbollah remains a significant player in the conflict with Israel, tied to Lebanon's stability and US-Iran negotiations. The group has shown its continued power despite previous setbacks. Despite setbacks, Hezbollah emerges as a key player, tied to Lebanon’s stability and US-Iran negotiations.

articles.title: Father and brother of suspect get 30 years’ jail for terrorism-related offences
articles.summary: A father and brother of a terrorism suspect were sentenced to 30 years in jail for their roles in a May 2024 attack in Johor Bahru, Malaysia, that killed two police officers.

articles.title: Alleged drug trafficking kingpin deported from Türkiye to India
articles.summary: Salim Dola, a 59-year-old alleged drug trafficking kingpin, was deported from Türkiye to India and handed over to the Narcotics Control Bureau. He will be produced before a court for further proceedings.

articles.title: Dozens of countries urge reopening of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Dozens of countries have called for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The appeal comes amid concerns over the strategic waterway's closure. Dozens of countries urge reopening of Strait of Hormuz rudaw. net.

articles.title: Trump reviews Iranian proposal aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: US President Trump met with his national security advisers to discuss an Iranian proposal aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The White House confirmed the meeting. The White House confirms Trump met his national security advisers on Monday to discuss the plan.

articles.title: 3 Scenarios, 2 Deadlines, 1 Iran War: Donald Trump Faces Point Of No Return
articles.summary: Donald Trump has three scenarios for a potential war with Iran, with two deadlines looming. The US President's options include a military strike, economic sanctions, or a diplomatic solution. The consequences of inaction are unclear.

articles.title: Afghan officials say Pakistani strikes killed 7, wounded 85 in first attacks since peace talks
articles.summary: Pakistani strikes on a university in Afghanistan killed 7 and wounded 85, according to Afghan officials. Pakistan's Ministry of Information and Broadcasting denied the reports, calling them a "blatant lie." The incident occurred despite ongoing peace talks.

articles.title: South Korea's central bank cites war uncertainty in cautious approach, minutes show
articles.summary: South Korea's central bank cited war uncertainty in its cautious approach to monetary policy. The bank's minutes showed policymakers weighing the risks of a potential conflict with North Korea. The decision reflects the country's fragile economic situation.

articles.title: Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador over ‘stolen’ grain shipments
articles.summary: Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador over grain shipments it claims were stolen. The move comes as Ukrainian drones hit Russia's Tuapse oil refinery, causing a massive fire. The diplomatic spat highlights ongoing tensions between Ukraine and Russia.

articles.title: Austrian goes on trial accused of plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert
articles.summary: A 21-year-old Austrian man went on trial for plotting an attack on Taylor Swift's concert. Prosecutors say he received training from Islamic State members on handling explosives. The trial marks a significant escalation in concerns over domestic terrorism in Austria.

articles.title: The evolving China-Pakistan space cooperation
articles.summary: China and Pakistan are cooperating in space, reflecting their strong ties. The ‘all-weather ties’ between China and Pakistan are reflected in their space cooperation as well. The evolving China-Pakistan space cooperation.

articles.title: Oil Giants Make $3,000 Every Second As US Families Struggle To Pay Bills
articles.summary: Oil giants are making $3,000 every second in profit while US families struggle to pay energy bills. The soaring profits of oil companies are contrasting with the financial strain on households.

articles.title: China pledges to strengthen supply chains, energy security as part of 'systemic response' to
articles.summary: China's Politburo has pledged to strengthen supply chains and energy security as part of a systemic response to economic challenges. The economy started the year better than expected. The Politburo, a top decision-making body of the ruling Communist Party, was cited as saying by state news agency Xinhua that the economy got off to a better-than-expected start this year.

articles.title: What’s happening on day 60 as diplomacy gathers pace?
articles.summary: The Trump team is reviewing an Iran peace plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Nuclear talks may be delayed to a later stage as diplomacy gains pace. Trump team reviews Iran peace plan to reopen Hormuz with nuclear talks potentially delayed to a later stage.

articles.title: Pirates Hijack Cargo Ship Near Somali Coast: UK Maritime Security
articles.summary: Pirates hijacked a cargo ship near the Somali coast. The 15-person crew includes two Indian nationals and 13 Syrians. The ship's 15-person crew comprises two Indian nationals and 13 Syrians.

articles.title: Trump administration fires entire National Science Board
articles.summary: The Trump administration fired the entire National Science Board effective immediately on April 24. No reason was provided for the action, according to board members. Board members were told on April 24 they were being ousted effective immediately, members Yolanda Gil and Keivan Stassun, said in emailed statements; no reason was provided for the action.

articles.title: नेतन्याहू को सत्ता से हटाने के लिए दो धुर विरोधी नेताओं ने हाथ मिलाया
articles.summary: नफ्ताली बेनेट और याएर लापिड ने अपनी पार्टियों का विलय कर 'याचाड' नामक नई पार्टी बनाई. यह पार्टी नेतन्याहू की सत्ता को चुनौती देगी. दोनों नेताओं का यह 연합 नेतन्याहू के लिए एक बड़ी चुनौती हो सकता है.

articles.title: चीन-रूस की मिसाइलों के सामने अमेरिकी रक्षा सिस्टम बेदम, गोल्डन डोम बनेगा गेम चेंजर!
articles.summary: पेंटागन ने माना कि अमेरिकी रक्षा सिस्टम चीन और रूस की मिसाइलों के सामने बेदम है. इस चुनौती का सामना करने के लिए गोल्डन डोम परियोजना पर काम किया जा रहा है. यह परियोजना अमेरिकी रक्षा क्षमताओं को बढ़ाने में मदद कर सकती है.

articles.title: ‘आतंकवाद के केंद्र अब सुरक्षित नहीं रहेंगे’, एससीओ बैठक में बोले रक्षा मंत्री राजनाथ सिंह
articles.summary: रक्षा मंत्री राजनाथ सिंह ने किर्गिस्तान के विजय चौक पर श्रद्धांजलि अर्पित की। वह शंघाई सहयोग संगठन की बैठक में भी शामिल होंगे। इस बैठक में क्षेत्रीय सुरक्षा और सहयोग के मुद्दों पर चर्चा होगी।

articles.title: सिर्फ 5-7 दिनों का कच्चा तेल शेष, पेट्रोलियम मंत्री परवेज मलिक ने कबूली लाचारी
articles.summary: पाकिस्तान के पेट्रोलियम मंत्री परवेज मलिक ने कहा है कि देश में केवल 5-7 दिनों का कच्चा तेल शेष है। इससे पाकिस्तान में ऊर्जा संकट गहराने की संभावना है। सरकार को ऊर्जा संकट से निपटने के लिए तत्काल कदम उठाने होंगे।

articles.title: मैक्सिको में ड्रग कार्टेल को बड़ा झटका: गिरफ्तार हुआ गिरोह का सबसे बड़ा नेता, अमेरिका ने रखा था

articles.title: ईरान होर्मुज खोलने को तैयार, अमेरिका से नाकेबंदी हटाने की मांग; ट्रंप ने प्रस्ताव ठुकराया
articles.summary: ईरान ने कहा है कि वह होर्मुज जलडमरूमध्य खोलने को तैयार है, अगर अमेरिका नाकेबंदी हटा दे। अमेरिकी राष्ट्रपति ट्रंप ने ईरान का प्रस्ताव ठुकरा दिया है। पश्चिम एशिया संघर्ष: ईरान होर्मुज खोलने को तैयार, अमेरिका से नाकेबंदी हटाने की मांग; ट्रंप ने प्रस्ताव ठुकराया Amar Ujala।

articles.title: पाकिस्तान ने अमेरिकी प्रतिबंध के बावजूद ईरान के लिए खोल दिया ये ट्रेड कॉरिडोर

articles.summary: भाजपा ने तृणमूल कांग्रेस को फंसाने के लिए एक रणनीति बनाई है. इस रणनीति के तहत, भाजपा ने महिला आरक्षण बिल का समर्थन किया है. इससे तृणमूल कांग्रेस को चुनाव में चुनौती मिल सकती है.

articles.summary: पाकिस्तान के पूर्व प्रधानमंत्री इमरान खान की आंखों की रोशनी पर खतरा मंडरा रहा है. उनकी चौथी आंख 수술 हुई है और अब उनकी हालत स्थिर बताई जा रही है. इमरान खान की आंखों की समस्या रेटिनल वेन ऑक्लूजन के कारण है.

articles.summary: पाकिस्तान ने संयुक्त राष्ट्र को बताया कि भारत द्वारा सिंधु जल संधि को निलंबित रखने से दक्षिण एशिया की शांति और सुरक्षा के लिए गंभीर परिणाम हो सकते हैं। इस फैसले से पाकिस्तान को एक साल में 상당 नुकसान हुआ है।
